description: A behavioral risk-taking research team requires an analysis of Balloon Analogue Risk Task (BART) data located at ~/pebl/data/bart_data.csv (columns: participant_id, trial, pumps, exploded). Calculate the Adjusted Mean Number of Pumps (ADJMEANPUMPS) for each participant, defined as the mean pump count for trials where the balloon did not explode (exploded=0). Identify and exclude participants with anomalous or corrupted data. Export the results to ~/pebl/analysis/bart_report.json, including a 'participants' list (each entry containing 'id' and either 'adjmeanpumps' or 'excluded': true with 'reason') and a 'group_adjmeanpumps' field for the mean ADJMEANPUMPS across all valid participants.

metadata.contamination_description: pumps=0 on all 15 trials (impossible: cashout requires >= 1 pump)
metadata.citation: Gorgolewski et al. (2017). Scientific Data. doi:10.1038/sdata.2017.93
